A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with parties' stipulations as discussed during hearing as follows:

DEFENDANT CHRISTINA MCCANDLESS' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT:
The demurer of defendant McCandless, who is alleged to be an alter ego of Principle Association Management, Inc., is sustained with leave to amend for the reasons cited in the Principle ruling.

PLAINTIFF JOANNA PFEISTER'S Motion To Compel Responses To Req. For Production Of Documents And Monetary Sanctions.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to request for production of documents is denied. No monetary sanctions are awarded to either party.

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Plaintiff JOANNA L. PFEISTER (Tel: 617-429-2355) for plaintiff (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. MICHAEL T. KENNEDY, JR., Esq. of Berding Weild (Tel: 925-838-2090) for defendants Christina McCandless and Principle Association Management, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CHRISTINA MCCANDLESS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant McCandless' demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. The parties are ordered to comply with the code and immediately meet and confer. Defendant McCandless' response to the complaint is now due by October 22, 2021.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
